Title: The evolution of betalain biosynthesis in Caryophyllales

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyrosine supply linked to betalain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Caryophyllales-specific ADHα is a duplicated ADH paralogue with relaxed sensitivity to tyrosine feedback inhibition. 2. ADHα produces higher tyrosine levels than canonical ADHβ in vitro and transient Nicotiana benthamiana assays. 3. Coupling ADHα with the full betalain pathway markedly increases betalain pigmentation, supporting a causal role in precursor supply for betalain evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq database mentioned for related studies; specific accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; associated with photoprotection, drought/salinity tolerance, and ROS scavenging in plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidative decarboxylation of arogenate to form tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an upstream precursor-supply module to boost engineered betalain production when stacked with core betalain biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme characterization; heterologous transient expression; metabolite profiling; pathway module coexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases tyrosine availability and enhances betalain accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with phenylalanine depletion, potentially affecting anthocyanin/phenylpropanoid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary metabolism interface with betalain pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control point for precursor availability
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review synthesizes evidence that ADHα predates betalain origin and is often lost or downregulated in anthocyanic Caryophyllales

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyrosine supply linked to betalain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADHβ represents the canonical tyrosine-sensitive ADH form in Caryophyllales. 2. Compared with ADHα, ADHβ is strongly feedback inhibited by tyrosine. 3. ADHβ therefore contributes less effectively to precursor supply for betalain production.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; associated with photoprotection, drought/salinity tolerance, and ROS scavenging in plants
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidative decarboxylation of arogenate to form tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less favorable than ADHα for metabolic engineering of betalains because of stronger feedback inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative in vitro and transient heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Canonical form with lower capacity to elevate tyrosine and betalain output
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary metabolism interface with betalain pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review treats ADHα/ADHβ as a contrasted functional pair central to tyrosine metabolism evolution

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD1 is highly expressed in betalain-producing beet tissues. 2. Functional studies show it catalyzes both tyrosine hydroxylation to L-DOPA and L-DOPA oxidation to cyclo-DOPA. 3. Loss or inactivation of BvCYP76AD1 abolishes red betacyanin formation, while transgenic expression restores pigmentation in yellow beet.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-associated antioxidant and photoprotective roles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tyrosine to L-DOPA and oxidizes L-DOPA to cyclo-DOPA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core gene for engineering red-violet betalain production; must be included to produce cyclo-DOPA-derived betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Beta vulgaris; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene silencing; transgenic complementation; mutant segregation; recombinant/heterologous expression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes betacyanin accumulation and is required for red pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Defective allele causes reduced pigment and increased tyrosine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for betacyanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Inducible system mentioned for regulatory tests; specific promoter NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents CYP76ADα lineage with dual activity

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MjCYP76AD3 is an orthologue of BvCYP76AD1 from Mirabilis jalapa. 2. Transposon-mediated mutation causes loss of betacyanin production. 3. Evidence supports a conserved role in cyclo-DOPA formation for betacyanin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-associated antioxidant and photoprotective roles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes L-DOPA to cyclo-DOPA; review also places CYP76ADα enzymes as dual-function with tyrosine hydroxylase activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate orthologue for engineering betacyanin formation in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transposon mutant analysis; pigment phenotype correlation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for betacyanin formation

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A quinoa CYP76AD1 orthologue was identified from a mutant screen. 2. The orthologue restores betalain pigmentation in a hypocotyl betalain-deficient mutant. 3. This supports conservation of CYP76AD1-like function across Caryophyllales.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-associated antioxidant and photoprotective roles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Supports conserved tyrosine/L-DOPA oxidation steps in betalain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for restoring or enhancing betalain pigmentation in quinoa breeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restores betalain pigmentation in mutant hypocotyls
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol not specified in review excerpt

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD5 catalyzes tyrosine hydroxylation to L-DOPA. 2. Unlike BvCYP76AD1, it lacks robust L-DOPA oxidase activity and therefore mainly supports yellow betalain formation. 3. It defines a CYP76ADβ clade specialized for the first committed step of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-associated antioxidant and photoprotective roles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selective engineering of yellow betalains or tuning hue when combined with cyclo-DOPA-forming enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes L-DOPA production and supports yellow betalain branch more than red branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only weak/very low betanidin detected, indicating limited cyclo-DOPA production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Member of CYP76ADβ clade retained in anthocyanic lineages

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD6 catalyzes tyrosine hydroxylation to L-DOPA. 2. It lacks robust cyclo-DOPA-forming activity and therefore differs functionally from BvCYP76AD1. 3. Coexpression with BvCYP76AD1 in tobacco generates mixed betalain hues, indicating a role in pigment color tuning.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-associated antioxidant and photoprotective roles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tyrosine to L-DOPA, with at most very weak side activity toward cyclo-DOPA formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning betalain hue ratios when stacked with BvCYP76AD1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous yeast expression; coexpression in tobacco; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports L-DOPA production and modulates orange-pink coloration when coexpressed with BvCYP76AD1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alone insufficient for strong betacyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important for selective production of yellow vs red betalains

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MjCYP76AD15 is a Mirabilis jalapa CYP76AD homologue with tyrosine hydroxylase activity. 2. It supports conservation of the CYP76ADβ-type function beyond Beta vulgaris. 3. It participates in the first committed step toward L-DOPA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; stress-associated antioxidant and photoprotective roles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering yellow betalain production in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous functional assays referenced in review
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports the initial committed step of betalain synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DODAα arose from a Caryophyllales LigB duplication and is the lineage associated with bona fide DODA activity. 2. It cleaves L-DOPA to 4,5-seco-DOPA, which spontaneously forms betalamic acid, the betalain chromophore. 3. DODAα is strongly associated with betalain-pigmented lineages and often lost or downregulated in anthocyanic reversals.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chromophore precursor of yellow and red betalains; pigmentation and stress-associated roles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes extradiol cleavage of L-DOPA between positions 4 and 5 to generate the betalamic acid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential core enzyme for engineering betalain synthesis in nonbetalain hosts
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Portulaca grandiflora; Antirrhinum majus; multiple Caryophyllales species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; complementation; biolistic transformation; pigment/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables betalamic acid formation and thereby both betaxanthin and betacyanin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: L-DOPA feeding used in heterologous background
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes DODAα neofunctionalization after duplication from DODAβ

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Portulaca grandiflora LigB homologue was isolated from differently colored isogenic lines. 2. Biolistic transformation and complementation in white petals confirmed its role in betalain production. 3. Introduction into nonbetalain backgrounds plus L-DOPA feeding was sufficient to trigger betalain formation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves L-DOPA to the precursor of betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core component for synthetic reconstruction of betalain pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subtractive cloning; biolistic transformation; complementation; heterologous expression; substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restores or induces betalain formation in white/nonbetalain tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the earliest plant DODA genes functionally validated

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvDODA1 is the beet DODA enzyme involved in betalain production. 2. It represents the active DODA paralogue contrasted with weakly active BvDODA2. 3. Comparative analysis with BvDODA2 identified residues necessary and sufficient for strong DODA activity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes extradiol cleavage of L-DOPA to produce the betalamic acid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred active DODA allele for metabolic engineering and structure-function guided optimization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; in vitro and in planta activity comparison; residue swapping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes betalain biosynthesis via efficient betalamic acid production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located near CYP76AD1 on chromosome 2 in beet, suggesting possible clustering

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvDODA2 is a beet DODAα paralogue with little or no apparent DODA activity. 2. It contrasts with BvDODA1 and enabled identification of seven residues required for strong DODA function. 3. It is unlikely to play a major role in betalain production in planta based on current evidence.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Shows only minor or negligible DODA activity relative to BvDODA1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for structure-function studies rather than direct deployment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; in vitro/in planta comparison; residue mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Little contribution to betalain formation under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A useful inactive/weak paralogue for defining catalytic residues

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Betanidin 5GT from Dorotheanthus bellidiformis transfers glucose to the 5-hydroxyl group of betanidin. 2. It mediates a late decorating step in betacyanin biosynthesis. 3. Phylogenetically it appears independently recruited from other glucosyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 5-hydroxyl group of betanidin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to stabilize and diversify engineered betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows only ~15% amino acid identity with betanidin 6GT, implying independent recruitment

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Betanidin 6GT from Dorotheanthus bellidiformis transfers glucose to the 6-hydroxyl group of betanidin. 2. It represents an alternative late glucosylation route for betacyanin diversification. 3. It is evolutionarily distinct from betanidin 5GT.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and structural diversification of betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 6-hydroxyl group of betanidin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for diversifying betalain structures in metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvGT is a beet UDP-glucosyltransferase highly similar to betanidin 5GT. 2. Its expression correlates with stress-induced betalain pigmentation. 3. Antisense knockdown reduces betalain pigmentation, although direct substrate specificity remains unconfirmed.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Correlates with betalain pigmentation induced by abiotic and biotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely glucosylates betanidin or related betalain intermediates, but precise substrate specificity is not confirmed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance stable betalain accumulation, but requires substrate validation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression correlation; antisense knockdown; pigment profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports betalain pigmentation; knockdown reduces pigment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Implicated rather than fully confirmed structural gene

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cDOPA5GT glucosylates cyclo-DOPA, supporting an alternative route to betanin and related glucosylated betacyanins. 2. Its expression correlates with betanin accumulation during flower development in Mirabilis jalapa. 3. The enzyme is conserved across Caryophyllales and has been incorporated into synthetic multigene betalain constructs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increased during flower development alongside betanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to cyclo-DOPA to form cyclo-DOPA 5-O-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important module for stable heterologous production of betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Mirabilis jalapa; Celosia cristata; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA isolation and enzyme characterization; developmental expression correlation; multigene pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of betanin and stable betacyanins

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Comparative transcriptomics in Mirabilis jalapa identified MjHGCT as a candidate acylation-related transferase. 2. Coexpression with betalain biosynthesis genes in Nicotiana benthamiana produced multiple acylated betanins. 3. This supports a role in generating structural diversity of betalains.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment diversification and likely altered stability/solubility
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Supports hydroxycinnamoyl transfer donor formation leading to acylated betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering novel acylated betalain derivatives with altered stability and color properties
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; heterologous coexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of acylated betacyanin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exact biochemical step and donor specificity not fully resolved in review
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as candidate acyltransferase-related enzyme in review

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; virus-induced gene silencing; yeast one-hybrid; inducible in vivo activation assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Inducible promoter/system used for in vivo upregulation assay; native BvDODA and BvCYP76AD1 promoters bound in yeast one-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Potentially related to MBW-type regulation, but direct canonical bHLH interaction is not supported for known anthocyanic bHLHs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in pigmented beet tissues; underexpressed in unpigmented varieties
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental and environmental pigment-regulatory context inferred; specific upstream signals NA
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Beet RNA-seq database used to identify candidate
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential betalain regulator that activates core structural genes and can induce red pigmentation in white beet roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in pigmented tissues; reduced in unpigmented beet varieties
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation; associated with photoprotection, antioxidant function, drought/salinity tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to subgroup 6 MYBs and appears coopted from anthocyanin regulators; lacks key canonical bHLH-interaction residues
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May contribute to anthocyanin pathway suppression after cooption, but direct pleiotropic costs are not established
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful regulatory lever for activating betalain synthesis in beet and potentially in heterologous systems when paired with structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes betalain accumulation and red coloration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvMYB1 was identified from beet RNA-seq as a highly expressed R2R3-MYB associated with pigmentation. 2. Overexpression induces red coloration, whereas silencing creates betalain-free sectors. 3. It binds promoters of BvDODA and BvCYP76AD1 and upregulates BvDODA, establishing it as a core regulator of betalain biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BvDODA and likely activates BvCYP76AD1-associated transcription through promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inducible expression system used in validation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; promoter binding assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Promoter of CYP76AD1 homologue containing W-box motif
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Potential interacting partner in broader MBW-like regulatory context was suggested but not proven
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified in Hylocereus polyrhizus by RNA-seq screen
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Putative additional regulator contributing to betalain structural gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is more limited than for BvMYB1; included because the review discusses it as a transcriptional regulator of betalain synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary regulator for enhancing betalain pathway expression once validated more fully
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes betalain biosynthesis through CYP76AD1-pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HpWRKY was identified in Hylocereus polyrhizus as a candidate betalain regulator. 2. It binds a W-box in the promoter of a CYP76AD1 homologue. 3. The study suggests WRKY participation in betalain transcriptional control.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds and likely activates CYP76AD1 homologue promoter

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Suggested possible component of a betalain regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in betacyanic tissue in Spinacia oleracea
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate partner linked to a major leaf-colour QTL explaining much of the color variation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence remains correlative; the review presents ORF11 as a potential additional regulatory partner rather than definitive regulator
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker or candidate regulator for leaf color breeding in spinach pending functional validation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with betacyanic leaf color variation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORF11 lies near a major spinach leaf-color QTL. 2. It is highly expressed in betacyanic tissue. 3. It is a plausible bHLH partner in betalain regulation, though direct function is unconfirmed.
